conversation.speaker: A
conversation.pronunciation: ˈwɛlkəm tə bʊks ɪn rɪˈvjuː | aʊə ɡɛst təˈdeɪ ɪz ʤɒn bæŋks, ði ˈɔːθə əv ðə bɛst ˈsɛlɪŋ njuː bʊk rɪˈwɔːdɪŋ səkˈsɛs
conversation.english: Welcome to Books in Review. Our guest today is John Banks, the author of the best selling new book Rewarding Success.
conversation.chinese: 欢迎来到《书评》。今天的嘉宾是约翰·班克斯，《回报成功》这本畅销新书的作者。

conversation.speaker: A
conversation.pronunciation: jɔː bʊk hæz soʊld tuː ˈmɪljən ˈkɒpiz | bət bɪˈfɔː wi dɪsˈkʌs waɪ ɪts ˈɡɛtɪŋ soʊ mʌʧ əˈtɛnʃən, lets tɔːk əˈbaʊt jɔː ˈbækgraʊnd | jɔːr ən iːkəˈnɒmɪst ænd spɛnt tuː ˈdɛkeɪdz ˈtiːʧɪŋ æt ˌjuːnɪˈvɜːsɪtiz?
conversation.english: Your book has sold 2 million copies, but before we discuss why it's getting so much attention, let's talk about your background. You're an economist and spent two decades teaching at universities?
conversation.chinese: 你的书已经卖出了两百万册，但在讨论它为什么如此受关注之前，先谈谈你的背景吧。你是一位经济学家，并在大学任教了二十年？

conversation.speaker: B
conversation.pronunciation: aɪ spɛnt 25 jɪəz əz ə prəˈfɛsə ˈækʧuəli | ænd ðɛn fə ðə lɑːst 10 jɪəz, aɪv wɜːkt əz ə pəˈlɪtɪkəl kənˈsʌltənt, ədˈvaɪzɪŋ ˌpɒlɪˈtɪʃənz æt ðə ˈnæʃənəl ˈlɛvl əˈbaʊt ˈprɒbləmz ɪn aʊə ˈkʌntri
conversation.english: I spent 25 years as a professor, actually. And then, for the last 10 years, I've worked as a political consultant, advising politicians at the national level about problems in our country.
conversation.chinese: 其实我当了 25 年教授。之后的 10 年，我一直担任政治顾问，向国家层面的政客提供有关国家问题的建议。

conversation.speaker: A
conversation.pronunciation: juː dɪsˈkʌs θriː əv ðoʊz ˈprɒbləmz ɪn ðə bʊk | ɪmˈpruːvɪŋ ˈpʌblɪk ˌɛdjuˈkeɪʃən, rɪˈdjuːsɪŋ aʊər ˈneɪʃənz hɛlθ keə ˈbɜːdən, ænd ɪnˈkriːsɪŋ ˈpɜːsənl ˈseɪvɪŋz | bət jɔːr aɪˈdɪəz əˈbaʊt ˌɛdjuˈkeɪʃən ɑː ðə moʊst ˌkɒntrəˈvɜːʃəl
conversation.english: You discuss three of those problems in the book—improving public education, reducing our nation's health care burden, and increasing personal savings. But your ideas about education are the most controversial.
conversation.chinese: 你在书中讨论了三个问题：提高公共教育水平、降低国家医疗负担，以及增加个人储蓄。但你关于教育的观点最具争议。

conversation.speaker: B
conversation.pronunciation: ˈæbsəluːtli | ə lɒt əv ˈpiːpl θɪŋk aɪm ˈtraɪɪŋ tə ˈpʌnɪʃ ˈstjuːdənts huː ɑːnt ˈduːɪŋ wɛl, wɛn ˈækʧuəli maɪ ɡoʊl ɪz tə ɡɪv ɔːl ˈstjuːdənts mɔːr ˌɪnsɛnˈtɪv tə səkˈsiːd
conversation.english: Absolutely. A lot of people think I'm trying to punish students who aren't doing well, when actually my goal is to give all students more incentive to succeed.
conversation.chinese: 确实。很多人认为我是在惩罚成绩不好的学生，但其实我的目标是给所有学生更多成功的动力。

conversation.speaker: A
conversation.pronunciation: aɪm nɒt ʃʊr aɪ əˈɡriː wɪð juː | jɔː prəˈpoʊzəl tə peɪ kæʃ rɪˈwɔːdz tə ˈstjuːdənts huː ɡɛt ɡʊd ɡreɪdz ɪz ə pəˈtɪkjələr ˈprɒbləm | wɒt əˈbaʊt ˈstjuːdənz huː doʊnt ɡɛt ɡʊd ɡreɪdz? ɪt siːmz laɪk jɔː ˈbleɪmɪŋ ðɛm fə nɒt səkˈsiːdɪŋ, wɛn pɔː ˈpɜːfɔːməns ɪn skuːl ˈɪznt ə tʃaɪldz fɔːlt
conversation.english: I'm not sure I agree with you. Your proposal to pay cash rewards to students who get good grades is a particular problem. What about students who don't get good grades? It seems like you're blaming them for not succeeding, when poor school performance isn't a child's fault.
conversation.chinese: 我不太同意。你提出给成绩好的学生发现金奖励，这本身就是个问题。那成绩不好的学生怎么办？这听起来像是在责怪他们不成功，但学生成绩差并不完全是孩子的错。

conversation.speaker: B
conversation.pronunciation: bət maɪ prəˈpoʊzəl ɪz nɒt ʤʌst tə rɪˈwɔːd ˈstjuːdənts wɪð ɡʊd ɡreɪdz, bət ˈɔːlsoʊ ˈstjuːdənts huː ʃoʊ ɪmˈpruːvmənt
conversation.english: But my proposal is not just to reward students with good grades, but also students who show improvement.
conversation.chinese: 但我的建议不仅奖励成绩好的学生，也奖励那些有进步的学生。

conversation.speaker: A
conversation.pronunciation: oʊˈkeɪ, əˈnʌðə ˈkrɪtɪsɪzəm əv ðə plæn ɪz ðə kæʃ rɪˈwɔːdz ðəmˈsɛlvz | weər wɪl ðə ˈmʌni kʌm frɒm?
conversation.english: Okay, another criticism of the plan is the cash rewards themselves. Where will the money come from?
conversation.chinese: 好吧，另一个争议点是这些现金奖励本身。资金从哪里来？

conversation.speaker: B
conversation.pronunciation: ɪf ˈstjuːdənts duː ˈbɛtə, wɪl spɛnd lɛs ɒn ˈskuːlɪŋ | soʊ ɪn ði ɛnd, ðə rɪˈwɔːdz wɪl peɪ fə ðəmˈsɛlvz
conversation.english: If students do better, we'll spend less on schooling. So, in the end, the rewards will pay for themselves.
conversation.chinese: 如果学生表现更好，我们在教育上的花费就会减少。所以最终，这些奖励会自我回本。

conversation.speaker: A
conversation.pronunciation: wɒt əˈbaʊt naʊ? haʊ wɪl wi fʌnd ðə rɪˈwɔːdz ɪn ðə ˈmiːntaɪm?
conversation.english: What about now? How will we fund the rewards in the meantime?
conversation.chinese: 那现在呢？在此期间我们又该如何筹措这笔奖励资金？

conversation.speaker: B
conversation.pronunciation: wɛl, baɪ ɪnˈkriːsɪŋ ˈtæksɪz ɔː ˈmuːvɪŋ ˈmʌni frəm ˈʌðər ˈɛriəz əv ðə ˈbʌdʒɪt ˈɪntuː ˌɛdjuˈkeɪʃən
conversation.english: Well, by increasing taxes or moving money from other areas of the budget into education.
conversation.chinese: 嗯，可以通过提高税收，或者从预算的其他部分调拨资金到教育领域。
